channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Cross-Family Speculative Decoding for Polish Language Models on Apple~Silicon: An Empirical Evaluation of Bielik~11B with UAG-Extended MLX-LM
item.description: arXiv:2604.16368v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates LLM inference by using a small draft model to propose k candidate tokens for a target model to verify. While effective for same-tokenizer pairs on high-bandwidth GPUs, its applicability to cross-family pairs with misma...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, study, context, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Foundational Study on Authorship Attribution of Japanese Web Reviews for Actor Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2604.16376v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study investigates the applicability of authorship attribution based on stylistic features to support actor analysis in threat intelligence. As a foundational step toward future application to dark web forums, we conducted experiments using Japan...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, embedding, arxiv, analysis, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Data Mixing for Large Language Models Pretraining: A Survey and Outlook
item.description: arXiv:2604.16380v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) rely on pretraining on massive and heterogeneous corpora, where training data composition has a decisive impact on training efficiency and downstream generalization under realistic compute and data budget constraints. Unli...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, arxiv, paper, RAG, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: QU-NLP at QIAS 2026: Multi-Stage QLoRA Fine-Tuning for Arabic Islamic Inheritance Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2604.16396v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Islamic inheritance law (ilm al-mawar{\i}th) presents a challenging domain for evaluating large language models' structured reasoning capabilities, requiring multi-step legal analysis, rule-based blocking decisions, and precise fractional calculations...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, reasoning, large language model, analysis, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: From Subsumption to Satisfiability: LLM-Assisted Active Learning for OWL Ontologies
item.description: arXiv:2604.16672v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In active learning, membership queries (MQs) allow a learner to pose questions to a teacher, such as ''Is every apple a fruit?'', to which the teacher responds correctly with yes or no. These MQs can be viewed as subsumption tests with respect to the ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, arxiv, framework, large language model, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Agentic Risk-Aware Set-Based Engineering Design
item.description: arXiv:2604.16687v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper introduces a multi-agent framework guided by Large Language Models (LLMs) to assist in the early stages of engineering design, a phase often characterized by vast parameter spaces and inherent uncertainty. Operating under a human-in-the-loo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, arxiv, RAG, paper, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: UniMamba: A Unified Spatial-Temporal Modeling Framework with State-Space and Attention Integration
item.description: arXiv:2604.16325v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multivariate time series forecasting is fundamental to numerous domains such as energy, finance, and environmental monitoring, where complex temporal dependencies and cross-variable interactions pose enduring challenges. Existing Transformer-based met...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, arxiv, context, framework, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SaFeR-Steer: Evolving Multi-Turn MLLMs via Synthetic Bootstrapping and Feedback Dynamics
item.description: arXiv:2604.16358v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: MLLMs are increasingly deployed in multi-turn settings, where attackers can escalate unsafe intent through the evolving visual-text history and exploit long-context safety decay. Yet safety alignment is still dominated by single-turn data and fixed-te...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, context, template, RAG, multimodal | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Matched-Learning-Rate Analysis of Attention Drift and Transfer Retention in Fine-Tuned CLIP
item.description: arXiv:2604.16410v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: CLIP adaptation can improve in-domain accuracy while degrading out-of-domain transfer, but comparisons between Full Fine-Tuning (Full FT) and LoRA are often confounded by different learning-rate conventions. We study how adaptation method and optimiza...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, arxiv, RAG, analysis, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Annotation Entropy Predicts Per-Example Learning Dynamics in LoRA Fine-Tuning
item.description: arXiv:2604.16332v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We find that LoRA fine-tuning exhibits un-learning on contested examples: items with high annotator disagreement show increasing loss during training, a qualitatively distinct pattern largely absent under full fine-tuning and consistent across all six...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, fine-tuning, example, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Query Channel: Information-Theoretic Limits of Masking-Based Explanations
item.description: arXiv:2604.16689v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Masking-based post-hoc explanation methods, such as KernelSHAP and LIME, estimate local feature importance by querying a black-box model under randomized perturbations. This paper formulates this procedure as communication over a query channel, where ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, framework, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
